---
title: "Why Major in Economics?"
subtitle: "Labor Market Outcomes by College Major"
author: "Clark University — Department of Economics"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    embed-resources: true
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.05
    code-fold: true
    fig-align: center
    css: slides.css
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px

# ── Color palette ──────────────────────────────────────────────
ECON_COLOR   = "#C8102E"   # Clark red — Economics highlight
OTHER_COLOR  = "#AAAAAA"   # Grey for all other majors
GRAD_COLOR   = "#1F6AA5"   # Blue for college grads
HS_COLOR     = "#E07B00"   # Orange for HS diploma

# ── Load data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. Outcomes by major
df = pd.read_excel("data/College-labor-data.xlsx",
                   sheet_name="outcomes by major", skiprows=10)
df.columns = ["Major", "Unemp", "Underemp", "Wage_Early", "Wage_Mid", "Grad_Degree"]
df = df.dropna(subset=["Major"])
df = df[df["Major"] != "Major"].reset_index(drop=True)
for col in ["Unemp", "Underemp", "Wage_Early", "Wage_Mid", "Grad_Degree"]:
    df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors="coerce")
df = df[df["Major"] != "Overall"]

# 2. Wages time series
wdf = pd.read_excel("data/College-labor-data.xlsx",
                    sheet_name="wages", skiprows=10)
wdf.columns = ["Year", "p25", "median", "p75", "hs_median"]
wdf = wdf.dropna(subset=["Year"])
wdf = wdf[wdf["Year"] != "Date"].reset_index(drop=True)
for col in ["p25", "median", "p75", "hs_median"]:
    wdf[col] = pd.to_numeric(wdf[col], errors="coerce")
wdf["Year"] = pd.to_datetime(wdf["Year"]).dt.year

# ── Helper: mark Economics ─────────────────────────────────────
def econ_color(series):
    return [ECON_COLOR if m == "Economics" else OTHER_COLOR for m in series]
```

## The College Wage Premium

College graduates consistently out-earn workers with only a high school diploma.

```{python}
#| label: fig-wage-premium
#| fig-cap: "Median annual earnings (2024 dollars). Source: NY Fed / CPS."

fig = go.Figure()

fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=wdf["Year"], y=wdf["median"],
    mode="lines", name="College — median",
    line=dict(color=GRAD_COLOR, width=3)
))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=wdf["Year"], y=wdf["p75"],
    mode="lines", name="College — 75th pct",
    line=dict(color=GRAD_COLOR, width=1.5, dash="dot"),
    fill="tonexty", fillcolor="rgba(31,106,165,0.10)"
))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=wdf["Year"], y=wdf["p25"],
    mode="lines", name="College — 25th pct",
    line=dict(color=GRAD_COLOR, width=1.5, dash="dot"),
    fill="tonexty", fillcolor="rgba(31,106,165,0.10)"
))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=wdf["Year"], y=wdf["hs_median"],
    mode="lines", name="High school — median",
    line=dict(color=HS_COLOR, width=3)
))

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Year",
    yaxis_title="Annual Earnings (USD)",
    yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.18),
    margin=dict(l=60, r=20, t=30, b=80),
    height=440,
    template="plotly_white",
    hovermode="x unified"
)
fig.show()
```

::: {.notes}
The shaded band shows the inter-quartile range for college graduates.
Even the 25th percentile of college earners tends to exceed the HS median.
:::

## Early-Career Wages by Major {.scrollable}

Where does **Economics** rank?

```{python}
#| label: fig-early-wages
df_sorted = df.sort_values("Wage_Early", ascending=True).copy()
df_sorted["color"] = [ECON_COLOR if m == "Economics" else OTHER_COLOR
                      for m in df_sorted["Major"]]
df_sorted["size"] = [14 if m == "Economics" else 8
                     for m in df_sorted["Major"]]

fig = go.Figure()

# Other majors
mask_other = df_sorted["Major"] != "Economics"
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=df_sorted.loc[mask_other, "Wage_Early"],
    y=df_sorted.loc[mask_other, "Major"],
    orientation="h",
    marker_color=OTHER_COLOR,
    name="Other majors",
    hovertemplate="%{y}: $%{x:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
))
# Economics
mask_econ = df_sorted["Major"] == "Economics"
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=df_sorted.loc[mask_econ, "Wage_Early"],
    y=df_sorted.loc[mask_econ, "Major"],
    orientation="h",
    marker_color=ECON_COLOR,
    name="Economics",
    hovertemplate="%{y}: $%{x:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
))

# Overall median line
overall_early = 58000
fig.add_vline(x=overall_early, line_dash="dash", line_color="#555",
              annotation_text="All-major median", annotation_position="top right")

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Median Early-Career Wage (USD)",
    xaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    showlegend=True,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.02, x=0),
    barmode="overlay",
    height=1400,
    margin=dict(l=230, r=20, t=40, b=60),
    template="plotly_white"
)
fig.show(config={"scrollZoom": False})
```

## Early vs. Mid-Career Wages

**Economics majors see strong wage growth** over their careers.

```{python}
#| label: fig-scatter-wages
df["is_econ"] = df["Major"] == "Economics"
df_other = df[~df["is_econ"]]
df_econ  = df[df["is_econ"]]

fig = go.Figure()

fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df_other["Wage_Early"], y=df_other["Wage_Mid"],
    mode="markers",
    marker=dict(color=OTHER_COLOR, size=7, opacity=0.7),
    name="Other majors",
    text=df_other["Major"],
    hovertemplate="<b>%{text}</b><br>Early: $%{x:,.0f}<br>Mid: $%{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
))

fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df_econ["Wage_Early"], y=df_econ["Wage_Mid"],
    mode="markers+text",
    marker=dict(color=ECON_COLOR, size=16, symbol="diamond"),
    name="Economics",
    text=["Economics"],
    textposition="top right",
    hovertemplate="<b>Economics</b><br>Early: $%{x:,.0f}<br>Mid: $%{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
))

# 45-degree reference line
lim_min = df[["Wage_Early", "Wage_Mid"]].min().min() * 0.95
lim_max = df[["Wage_Early", "Wage_Mid"]].max().max() * 1.05
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=[lim_min, lim_max], y=[lim_min, lim_max],
    mode="lines", line=dict(dash="dot", color="#999", width=1),
    showlegend=False, hoverinfo="skip"
))

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Median Early-Career Wage (USD)",
    yaxis_title="Median Mid-Career Wage (USD)",
    xaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.15),
    height=480,
    margin=dict(l=80, r=20, t=30, b=80),
    template="plotly_white"
)
fig.show()
```

::: {.fragment}
> The dotted line marks equal early- and mid-career wages. Points above it grow the most — hover to explore!
:::

## Underemployment Rate by Major {.scrollable}

*Underemployment* = working in a job that doesn't require a college degree.

```{python}
#| label: fig-underemployment
df_sorted_u = df.sort_values("Underemp", ascending=True).copy()

fig = go.Figure()

mask_other = df_sorted_u["Major"] != "Economics"
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=df_sorted_u.loc[mask_other, "Underemp"],
    y=df_sorted_u.loc[mask_other, "Major"],
    orientation="h",
    marker_color=OTHER_COLOR,
    name="Other majors",
    hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>"
))
mask_econ = df_sorted_u["Major"] == "Economics"
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=df_sorted_u.loc[mask_econ, "Underemp"],
    y=df_sorted_u.loc[mask_econ, "Major"],
    orientation="h",
    marker_color=ECON_COLOR,
    name="Economics",
    hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>"
))

# Overall median line
overall_underemp = df["Underemp"].median()
fig.add_vline(x=overall_underemp, line_dash="dash", line_color="#555",
              annotation_text=f"Median ({overall_underemp:.1f}%)",
              annotation_position="top right")

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Underemployment Rate (%)",
    showlegend=True,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.02, x=0),
    barmode="overlay",
    height=1400,
    margin=dict(l=230, r=20, t=40, b=60),
    template="plotly_white"
)
fig.show(config={"scrollZoom": False})
```

## Economics vs. Business Majors

Same wages, fewer grad-school degrees needed — or: *equal* grad-school investment, far higher wages.

```{python}
#| label: fig-econ-vs-business
comps = ["Economics", "Finance", "Business Analytics",
         "Accounting", "Marketing", "General Business",
         "Business Management"]

dfc = df[df["Major"].isin(comps)].copy().reset_index(drop=True)
dfc["color"] = [ECON_COLOR if m == "Economics" else OTHER_COLOR
                for m in dfc["Major"]]
dfc["label_pos"] = ["top center" if m == "Economics" else "bottom center"
                    for m in dfc["Major"]]

fig = go.Figure()

# Other business majors
for _, row in dfc[dfc["Major"] != "Economics"].iterrows():
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=[row["Grad_Degree"]],
        y=[row["Wage_Mid"]],
        mode="markers+text",
        marker=dict(
            color=OTHER_COLOR, size=row["Wage_Early"] / 4000,
            line=dict(color="white", width=1)
        ),
        text=[row["Major"]],
        textposition="bottom center",
        textfont=dict(size=11),
        showlegend=False,
        hovertemplate=(
            f"<b>{row['Major']}</b><br>"
            f"Grad degree: {row['Grad_Degree']:.1f}%<br>"
            f"Early wage: ${row['Wage_Early']:,.0f}<br>"
            f"Mid wage: ${row['Wage_Mid']:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
        )
    ))

# Economics
row = dfc[dfc["Major"] == "Economics"].iloc[0]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=[row["Grad_Degree"]],
    y=[row["Wage_Mid"]],
    mode="markers+text",
    marker=dict(
        color=ECON_COLOR, size=row["Wage_Early"] / 4000,
        symbol="diamond",
        line=dict(color="white", width=2)
    ),
    text=["<b>Economics</b>"],
    textposition="top center",
    textfont=dict(size=13, color=ECON_COLOR),
    showlegend=False,
    hovertemplate=(
        f"<b>Economics</b><br>"
        f"Grad degree: {row['Grad_Degree']:.1f}%<br>"
        f"Early wage: ${row['Wage_Early']:,.0f}<br>"
        f"Mid wage: ${row['Wage_Mid']:,.0f}<extra></extra>"
    )
))

fig.add_annotation(
    x=0.5, y=-0.22, xref="paper", yref="paper",
    text="Bubble size = median early-career wage",
    showarrow=False, font=dict(size=11, color="#777")
)

fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Share with Graduate Degree (%)",
    yaxis_title="Median Mid-Career Wage (USD)",
    yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    height=460,
    margin=dict(l=80, r=20, t=30, b=80),
    template="plotly_white"
)
fig.show()
```

::: {.fragment}
Economics achieves the **highest mid-career wage** ($115K) among all business-related majors.
Early-career wage ($72K) matches Finance — **before accounting for grad school leverage**.
:::

## The Economics Advantage — At a Glance

```{python}
#| label: fig-summary-radar
econ_row  = df[df["Major"] == "Economics"].iloc[0]
all_ranks = {}

for col, ascending in [("Wage_Early", False), ("Wage_Mid", False),
                        ("Underemp", True), ("Unemp", True)]:
    ranked = df[col].rank(ascending=ascending, method="min")
    all_ranks[col] = ranked[df["Major"] == "Economics"].values[0]

n = len(df)

categories = [
    "Early-Career Wage",
    "Mid-Career Wage",
    "Low Underemployment",
    "Low Unemployment"
]
# Convert rank to percentile score (higher = better)
scores = [
    round((n - all_ranks["Wage_Early"]) / (n - 1) * 100, 1),
    round((n - all_ranks["Wage_Mid"])   / (n - 1) * 100, 1),
    round((n - all_ranks["Underemp"])   / (n - 1) * 100, 1),
    round((n - all_ranks["Unemp"])      / (n - 1) * 100, 1),
]

fig = go.Figure(go.Scatterpolar(
    r=scores + [scores[0]],
    theta=categories + [categories[0]],
    fill="toself",
    fillcolor=f"rgba(200,16,46,0.25)",
    line=dict(color=ECON_COLOR, width=3),
    name="Economics"
))

# Add "average" benchmark at 50
fig.add_trace(go.Scatterpolar(
    r=[50, 50, 50, 50, 50],
    theta=categories + [categories[0]],
    mode="lines",
    line=dict(color="#999", dash="dot", width=1.5),
    name="All-major average"
))

fig.update_layout(
    polar=dict(
        radialaxis=dict(visible=True, range=[0, 100],
                        tickvals=[25, 50, 75, 100],
                        ticktext=["25th", "50th", "75th", "100th"])
    ),
    showlegend=True,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.10),
    height=460,
    margin=dict(l=80, r=80, t=40, b=80),
    template="plotly_white"
)
fig.show()
```

::: {.fragment}
Each axis shows Economics' **percentile rank** relative to all majors (higher = better).
:::

## Key Takeaways

::: {.incremental}
- **College pays off** — the wage premium over high school has held for 35+ years
- **Economics ranks in the top quartile** for both early- and mid-career wages
- **Low underemployment** — Economics graduates typically work in degree-requiring jobs
- **Graduate school option** — ~40% of Economics grads pursue further education, keeping options open
:::

::: {.fragment}
### Want to know more?
Visit the Economics Department or speak to your advisor.
Data source: [NY Fed — Labor Market for Recent College Graduates](https://www.newyorkfed.org/research/college-labor-market/index)
:::
